info.name: Bahrain Delivery Backend API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Bahrain Delivery Backend.

**Base URL:** `{{baseUrl}}` → set to `http://localhost:3000`

**Auth:** All protected routes require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

**Roles:** Customer | Manager | Delivery_Person | Admin

**Phone format:** Ethiopian E.164 e.g. `+251912345678`

item.name: 🔐 Auth
item.description:
Authentication endpoints — OTP-based signup, login, password reset, and Telegram auth.

Typical signup flow:
1. POST /signup → get verificationId
2. POST /verifySignupOTP → get JWT token

Typical login flow:
1. POST /login → get JWT token (or OTP if not verified)

request.description:
**Step 1 of signup.** Sends a 6-digit OTP to the provided Ethiopian phone number via AfroMessage SMS.

- If the user already exists and is verified → error 400
- Save the returned `verificationId` for the next step
- OTP is valid for 5 minutes

**Step 2 of signup.** Verifies the OTP, creates the user account (role=Customer), and returns a JWT token.

- `verificationId` is from step 1
- `password` minimum 4 characters
- `passwordConfirm` must match `password`
- **Copy the returned `token` into the `{{token}}` variable**

Login with phone number and password.

- Returns JWT token on success
- If phone not verified → sends OTP instead of token
- **Copy the returned `token` into `{{token}}`**

Send an OTP to any phone number. Used for re-sending expired OTPs.

Returns: `verificationId` needed for verification endpoints.

Verify an OTP code. On success:
- Marks phone as verified
- Returns JWT token immediately

Used for login-via-OTP and phone verification flows.

Send a password reset OTP to the phone number. Phone must belong to an existing account.

Returns `verificationId` needed for the reset step.

Reset password using the OTP received on the phone. On success updates the password and returns a new JWT token.

- `password` minimum 4 characters
- `passwordConfirm` must match `password`

Update the currently logged-in user's password. Requires valid auth token.

- Minimum 4 characters
- `passwordConfirm` must match `password`
- Returns new JWT token

Authenticate or register a user via Telegram.

- If user does NOT exist → creates new Customer account with a random 6-digit password (returned in response)
- If user EXISTS → logs in and returns JWT
- `telegramId` and `phone` are required
Exchange a Telegram ID for a JWT token. Used by the Telegram bot to get an API token for a previously registered user without going through OTP.

Google Play compliance endpoint. Soft-marks the account for deletion.

- `phone` and `email` are required
- Returns 200 even if user doesn't exist (security: no user enumeration)
- Admin must process the deletion manually

item.name: 👤 Me (Current User)
item.description: Endpoints for the currently authenticated user — profile, password change, and address management. All require Bearer token.

request.description:
Get the full profile of the currently authenticated user.

- If role is Manager → also returns their associated restaurant (`id` + `name`)
- Token is read from `Authorization` header or `jwt` cookie

Update the current user's first name, last name, or profile picture.

- **Role must be Customer**
- Use `multipart/form-data` when uploading a profile picture (field: `profilePicture`)
- Cannot update password or phone here
- Image is uploaded to Cloudinary (folder: `profile_pictures`)

Add a new address to the current user account.

- Maximum **3 addresses** per user
- `label` must be one of: `Home` | `Work` | `Other`
- `location.lat` and `location.lng` are required
- If `isDefault: true`, all other addresses are unset as default
- Stored as GeoJSON Point `[lng, lat]`
Get all saved addresses of the currently authenticated user. Returns an array of address subdocuments including their `_id`, `label`, `location`, and `isDefault` flag.

Edit an existing address by its subdocument `_id`.

- Only provided fields are updated
- `label` must be `Home` | `Work` | `Other` if provided
- Providing `isDefault: true` unsets all other default addresses

Delete an address by its subdocument `_id`.

- If the deleted address was the default, the **first remaining address** is automatically promoted to default
- Returns updated addresses array

item.name: 🛡️ Users (Admin)
item.description: Admin-only user management endpoints. All require Admin role except `Delivery Person Signup` which is public.

request.description:
Get all users. Both query params are optional.

- `role` filter: `Customer` | `Manager` | `Delivery_Person` | `Admin`
- `active` filter: `true` | `false`

**Requires Admin role**

Create a new user as Admin. Sends OTP to verify phone. Default password is `1234`.

**Role-specific required fields:**
- `Manager` → `fcnNumber` required
- `Delivery_Person` → `fcnNumber` + `deliveryMethod` required

Optionally upload `profilePicture` as file (multipart).

**Requires Admin role**

query.description: Search by phone (disable id if using this)

request.description:
Search for a user using one or more query params:

- `?id=USER_ID` — find by MongoDB _id
- `?phone=+251912345678` — find by phone number
- `?active=false` — filter by active status

At least one param is required.

**Requires Admin role**

Update a user by ID. All fields are optional.

- Changing `phone` triggers a new OTP send and marks phone as unverified
- Changing `role` to `Delivery_Person` requires `fcnNumber` + `deliveryMethod`
- Supports profile picture upload (multipart)

**Requires Admin role**

Hard-delete a user by ID. Returns **204 No Content** on success.

**Requires Admin role**

Activate an **inactive** user account.

- Only works on accounts where `active: false`
- Typically used after reviewing a Delivery Person signup
- Returns 404 if user is not found or already active

**Requires Admin role**

Public self-registration for delivery persons.

- Account is created as `active: false` — login is blocked until Admin activates it
- Default password: `1234`
- Optional: `profilePicture` image upload (multipart)

**Required fields:** `phone`, `firstName`, `lastName`, `fcnNumber`, `deliveryMethod`

item.name: 🏪 Restaurants
item.description: Restaurant management. Public GET endpoints. Admin for create. Manager or Admin for update/delete.

request.description: Public. Returns all active restaurants.

Create a new restaurant. Requires Admin role.

- Upload image as `multipart/form-data`, field name: `image`
- Image is uploaded to Cloudinary
- `managerId` must be a User ID with role `Manager`

item.name: Get All Restaurants — Admin List 🔒 Admin

request.description: Get complete list of **all** restaurants including inactive ones. Requires Admin role.
Public. Returns all restaurants with their aggregated order statistics.

Assign a Manager-role user to a restaurant. The user must already have the `Manager` role.

**Requires Admin role**
Update the GPS coordinates of a restaurant. Requires Admin or Manager role.
Get all restaurants where `managerId` matches the current user. Requires Manager or Admin role.
Get restaurants sorted by distance from the provided coordinates. Requires auth.
Public. Get a single restaurant by MongoDB ID.
Update a restaurant by ID. Supports image re-upload. Requires Manager or Admin role.
Delete a restaurant by ID. Requires Manager or Admin role.
Activate an inactive restaurant by ID. Requires Admin role.
Get a restaurant with all its menus and food items fully populated in a single request. Ideal for customer-facing restaurant screens.

item.name: 🍔 Foods
item.description: Food item management. Public GET. Admin or Manager for create/update/delete.

request.description: Public. Returns all food items across all restaurants.

Create a new food item. Requires Admin or Manager role.

- Upload image as `multipart/form-data`, field name: `imageCover`
- Image is uploaded to Cloudinary
Public. Get all food items belonging to a specific menu by its ID.
Public. Get a single food item by its MongoDB ID.
Update a food item by ID. Supports image re-upload. Requires Admin or Manager role.
Delete a food item by ID. Requires Admin or Manager role.

item.name: 🍽️ Food Menus
item.description: Menu category management. Public GET. Admin/Manager for write operations.

request.description: Public. Returns all food menus.
Public. Get a single menu by its ID.
Public. Returns all menus belonging to a specific restaurant.
Create a new menu category for a restaurant. Requires Admin or Manager role.
Update a menu by ID. Requires Admin or Manager role.
Delete a menu by ID. Requires Admin or Manager role.

Bulk-create menus and their food items in a single request. Useful for initial restaurant setup.

**Requires Admin role**

item.name: Get All Foods by Menu ID (alt route)

request.description: Public. Alternative route to fetch all foods for a menu. Used by customer-facing app screens.

item.name: 📦 Orders
item.description: Order lifecycle management. Customers place orders, Restaurants update status, Delivery Persons pick up and deliver.

request.description:
Place a new order. Initializes a Chapa payment and returns a `checkout_url`.

- `orderType`: `Delivery` | `Pickup`
- `items`: array of `{ foodId, quantity }`
- `deliveryAddress`: required for Delivery orders
- Returns `checkout_url` — redirect customer here to complete payment
Estimate the delivery fee before placing an order. Based on distance between restaurant and delivery address.
Get the current platform service fee configuration.

⚠️ **Do not call manually in production.** This webhook is called automatically by Chapa's servers after a payment is completed or failed.

Verifies payment signature and updates order status to `Confirmed`.
Get all orders placed by the currently authenticated user. Results are cached in Redis.
Get orders associated with the authenticated user's phone number.

item.name: Get All Orders by Status 🔒 Admin

variable.description: Pending | Confirmed | Preparing | Cooked | Picked_Up | Delivered | Cancelled

request.description:
Get all orders across the platform filtered by status. Requires Admin role.

**Status values:** `Pending` | `Confirmed` | `Preparing` | `Cooked` | `Picked_Up` | `Delivered` | `Cancelled`
Delivery person accepts a cooked order for delivery. Assigns the current user as the delivery person for the order.

Update the status of an order. Allowed transitions by role:

- **Manager:** `Confirmed` → `Preparing` → `Cooked`
- **Delivery_Person:** `Picked_Up` → `Delivered`
- **Admin:** any transition
Customer confirms delivery by entering the 6-digit code shown by the delivery person. This completes the order and triggers payment disbursement.
Delivery person confirms they have picked up the order from the restaurant using the restaurant pickup code.
Get all orders for a specific restaurant. Results are cached in Redis. Requires Manager or Admin role.
Get all orders with `Cooked` status for the manager's restaurant. Used by restaurant display screens.
Get all cooked orders available for pickup by delivery persons. Used by delivery apps to show a list of available jobs.
Returns the **count** of cooked orders waiting for pickup. Used for notification badges in the delivery app.

item.name: Get My Active Delivery Orders 🔒 Delivery

request.description: Get all active orders currently assigned to the authenticated delivery person.
Get completed delivery history for the currently authenticated delivery person.
Get all restaurants with their aggregated order statistics. Requires Admin role.

item.name: 💰 Balance & Withdrawals
item.description: Wallet management for delivery persons and managers. Includes balance checking, withdrawal requests, and transaction history.

request.description:
⚠️ **Public webhook — do not call manually.** Called by Chapa automatically after a transfer/withdrawal is approved or rejected.

Updates the withdrawal record status and notifies the user.
Get current wallet balance. Requires `Delivery_Person` or `Manager` role.
Get the withdrawal form data — available banks list + current balance. Call this before showing the withdrawal screen. Requires `Delivery_Person` or `Manager` role.

Submit a withdrawal request via Chapa transfer. Requires `Delivery_Person` or `Manager` role.

- `bankCode` from the initialize-withdraw banks list
- `accountNumber` — bank account number
- `amount` — must not exceed current balance
Get the full credit/debit transaction history of the authenticated user's wallet. Requires `Delivery_Person` or `Manager` role.

Get all withdrawal requests filtered by requester type. Requires Admin role.

- `:requesterType`: `Delivery_Person` | `Manager`

item.name: 💬 Reviews
item.description: Text reviews for restaurants. Supports direct and nested restaurant routes. One review per user per restaurant.

request.description: Public. Returns all reviews across all restaurants, each populated with restaurant and user details.
Public. Get a single review by its ID with restaurant and user populated.

Create a new review for a restaurant. Requires auth.

The `user` field is automatically set from the token. When using the nested route `/restaurants/:restaurantId/reviews`, `restaurant` is also auto-set from the URL.
Update your own review. Only the review **owner** can update it — returns 403 otherwise.
Delete your own review by ID. Only the review **owner** can delete it — returns 403 otherwise.
Public. Get all reviews for a specific restaurant using the nested URL pattern.
Create a review using the nested URL. `restaurantId` is auto-injected from the URL, `user` from the token.

item.name: ⭐ Ratings
item.description: Integer star ratings (1–5) for restaurants. One rating per user per restaurant enforced by a unique index. Automatically recalculates restaurant `ratingAverage` and `ratingQuantity` on every create/update/delete.

request.description: Public. Returns all ratings across all restaurants. Each rating is auto-populated with user details (`firstName`, `lastName`, `email`).
Public. Get a single rating by its MongoDB ID.

Submit a star rating for a restaurant. Requires auth.

- `rating`: **integer 1–5** (required)
- `comment`: optional, 10–1000 characters
- `restaurant`: required (MongoDB ID)
- **One rating per user per restaurant** — duplicate returns error 400
- Automatically updates restaurant `ratingAverage` and `ratingQuantity`

Update your own rating. Only the rating **owner** can update it — returns 403 otherwise.

Changing the rating value automatically recalculates the restaurant's average.

Delete your own rating. Only the rating **owner** can delete it — returns 403 otherwise.

Restaurant `ratingAverage` and `ratingQuantity` are recalculated automatically.
Public. Get all ratings for a specific restaurant using the nested URL pattern.
Create a rating using the nested URL. The `restaurant` field is auto-injected from the URL param — no need to include it in the body.

item.name: 🚚 Deliveries
item.description: Delivery assignment management. Links delivery persons to orders.

request.description: Assign a delivery person to an order. Requires auth.
Public. Returns all delivery records.
Cancel an active delivery assignment. Requires auth.
Public. Get a single delivery record by ID.
Update a delivery record by ID.
Delete a delivery record by ID.

item.name: 📱 SMS
item.description: Bulk SMS sending via AfroMessage. Requires auth.

request.description:
Send the **same message** to multiple phone numbers. Requires auth.

- `phones`: array of E.164 phone numbers
- `message`: the SMS text to send

Send **different personalized messages** to different recipients. Requires auth.

- `recipients`: array of `{ phone, message }` objects

item.name: 🏢 Restaurant Applications
item.description:
Restaurant owner self-registration and Admin review workflow.

Flow: Owner submits application → Admin reviews → Admin approves/rejects

request.description:
Submit a new restaurant registration application. Requires auth (any role).

**Use `multipart/form-data`.**

**Required files:**
- `license` (image) — business license

**Optional files based on `menuType`:**
- `menuImage` — if `menuType=image`
- `menuFile` — if `menuType=file`

**`menuType` values:** `text` | `image` | `file`

Duplicate prevention: one application per userId, phone, TIN, or FCN.

Get all restaurant applications with pagination. Requires Admin role.

- `status`: `pending` | `approved` | `rejected` (optional filter)
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Results sorted by newest first
Get a single restaurant application by ID, populated with the applicant user details. Requires Admin role.

Approve a restaurant application. Sets status to `approved`. Requires Admin role.

Optionally add `adminNotes` to explain the decision.

Reject a restaurant application. Sets status to `rejected`. Requires Admin role.

Add `adminNotes` to explain the rejection reason to the applicant.

item.name: 🎯 Promotions
item.description: Banner/promotional card management for the app home screen. Public GET. Admin-only for create/update/delete.

query.description: Return flat array instead of nested object

request.description:
Public. Returns all promotions sorted by `order`, then `priority` desc, then `createdAt` desc.

**Query params:**
- `isActive`: `true` | `false` (optional filter)
- `flat`: `true` → returns flat array (default is nested `{ promotions: [] }`)

Create a new promotional banner. Requires Admin role.

- Upload banner image with field name `imageCover` (multipart)
- `type`: `banner` | `popup` | `card`
- `target`: `all` | `Customer` | `Manager`
- `order`: display position index (lower = first)
- `priority`: tiebreaker (higher = first)
- `startDate` / `endDate`: ISO date strings (optional)

formdata.value: Get 50% off on all orders above 5 BHD

request.description: Update a promotion by ID. Only provided fields are updated. Supports image re-upload. Requires Admin role.
Delete a promotion by ID. Requires Admin role.
Reorder promotions by providing an ordered array of promotion IDs. The array index becomes the `order` value (0 = displayed first). Requires Admin role.

item.name: 🧪 Test / Dev
item.description: Development and debugging utilities. Do not use in production.

request.description:
Emits a test `order:cooked` WebSocket event to the `deliveries` room.

Useful for verifying that:
- Socket.io is running
- Delivery app clients are connected to the correct room
- Real-time events are received correctly

**Do not use in production.**
Returns server health status including uptime, memory usage, environment, Redis connection status and latency.
